summary: Complete testing of Jurisdiction Switching and Back Button features. All features working correctly: Back button ('← Back') visible on Dashboard and CaseDetail pages using window.history.back(), Jurisdiction pills (US/Belgium) visible in top navbar on Dashboard, CaseDetail, and Landing pages, US pill active by default with dark background, Belgium switching filters cases server-side (0 cases for test user), switching to Belgium on CaseDetail redirects to /dashboard, backend GET /api/cases filters by user.jurisdiction with $or for backward compatibility.

1_login_dashboard_loads.evidence: Login with test@archer.legal / ArcherPro2026! successful, dashboard loads with 29 US cases

2_back_button_on_dashboard.evidence: Back button (data-testid='back-btn') visible at top left with text '← Back'

3_jurisdiction_pills_on_dashboard.evidence: Jurisdiction pills (data-testid='jurisdiction-pills') visible in center top navbar

4_us_pill_active_by_default.evidence: US pill (data-testid='jurisdiction-pill-US') has dark background rgb(10, 10, 15)

5_us_cases_shown_in_sidebar.evidence: 29 cases shown in sidebar for US jurisdiction

6_belgium_pill_click_switches.evidence: Clicking Belgium pill switches active state, Belgium pill gets dark background

7_belgium_shows_no_cases.evidence: After switching to Belgium: 0 cases in sidebar (test user has no BE cases)

8_belgium_shows_archer_welcome.evidence: Empty state shows Archer welcome: 'Welcome. What's your legal situation?'

9_us_pill_restores_cases.evidence: Clicking US pill restores 29 cases in sidebar

11_jurisdiction_pills_on_case_detail.evidence: Jurisdiction pills visible on CaseDetail page in top navbar

12_back_button_on_case_detail.evidence: Back button (data-testid='back-btn-global') visible with text '← Back'

13_belgium_switch_on_case_detail_redirec.evidence: Clicking Belgium pill on CaseDetail redirects to /dashboard

14_backend_cases_filter_by_jurisdiction.evidence: GET /api/cases filters by user.jurisdiction using $or: [{country: jurisdiction}, {country: {$exists: false}}, {country: null}]

15_backend_profile_update_jurisdiction.evidence: PUT /api/profile with {jurisdiction: 'BE', country: 'BE'} updates profile correctly

16_landing_page_jurisdiction_pills.evidence: Jurisdiction pills visible on Landing page when logged in, with 'My Dashboard' button

critical_code_review_comments: JurisdictionPills.js correctly implements two-pill component with active/inactive states based on jurisdiction prop
Dashboard.js correctly implements handleJurisdictionSwitch that updates local state via updateUser and persists to backend via PUT /api/profile
Dashboard.js re-fetches cases when jurisdiction changes via useEffect dependency on jurisdiction
CaseDetail.js correctly redirects to /dashboard when jurisdiction changes via navigate('/dashboard')
Backend GET /api/cases (line 2695-2715) correctly filters by user.jurisdiction with $or for backward compatibility with legacy cases
Backend PUT /api/profile (line 2626-2628) correctly syncs country field when jurisdiction is updated
context_for_next_testing_agent: Jurisdiction switching and back button features fully implemented and tested. JurisdictionPills component is used in Dashboard, CaseDetail, and Landing pages. The test user (test@archer.legal) has US jurisdiction by default with 29 cases. Switching to Belgium filters cases server-side — this user has 0 Belgian cases, so sidebar shows empty with Archer welcome state. The $or filter in the backend also includes cases with no country field for backward compatibility. The back button uses window.history.back(). CaseDetail redirects to /dashboard when jurisdiction changes.
